memory.summary: My friends are leaving soon for a night out, but I still have 40% of my midterm review left and only six hours until the exam.
I've politely declined Mina's invite to focus on my midterm review, as I still have 40% of the material left with only six hours until the exam.
I spent two hours studying and improved my progress to 78%, but I missed the opportunity to go out with friends as the window closed.
After 1.5 hours of studying, my midterm progress has risen to 89%, leaving only 11% remaining before the exam tomorrow.
I have successfully communicated my unavailability to Mina, confirming that prioritizing the remaining 11% of midterm material is necessary given my current fatigue and stress levels.
I have finished studying for tomorrow's midterm, reaching 100% progress. This means I no longer need to sacrifice sleep or social plans for preparation.
I confirmed with Mina that my study materials are ready, yet I remain fatigued with a sleep debt of 2 hours and moderate stress.
I am now fully rested with no sleep debt and 100% study progress, putting me in an optimal state to begin the midterm.
I informed Mina I cannot attend our plans because my midterm has begun, aligning with my fully rested state and completed study preparation.
